title: EEG recordings during sham neurofeedback in virtual reality

funding_references.funder_name: This work was supported by CONICET (Argentina) and Universidad Nacional de Entre Ríos.

description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings acquired during a sham neurofeedback experiment conducted in virtual reality, investigating how feedback valence modulates alpha-band activity during attentional tasks. Participants received positive, negative, or control feedback while performing an attentional task, with additional resting-state recordings (eyes open and closed). Data were collected using a 32-channel SynAmps RT system and converted to BIDS format.
methods_description: EEG signals were recorded using a 32-channel SynAmps RT amplifier (Compumedics NeuroScan Inc.) with Ag/AgCl passive electrodes mounted on an elastic cap following the extended 10–20 international system. Visual and auditory stimuli were presented via a VR headset developed in Unity3D, synchronized with event markers sent to the EEG system. Participants completed four conditions: positive feedback, negative feedback, control (no feedback), and resting-state (eyes open and closed, 1.5 minutes each). High-pass filter 0.1 Hz, low-pass filter 500 Hz, and notch filter 50 Hz were applied.
